titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 754

p.seg: Henry the earl, son of the king of Scotland, to bishops, abbots, priors, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, officials, and all worthy men of the entire realm of Scotland, greeting.
Be it know to you all that

Swain the priest, in front of me and in my presence, quitclaimed to the monks of Coldingham, and gave back to them at Berwick, Fishwick with all things pertaining to it in lands and waters; and half of Prenderguest; and the land which he had in Coldingham and Lumsdaine.
I therefore will that, with every vexatious action of the aforesaid Swain removed, they hold and possess their aforenamed lands in peace.
As witness the chancellor, Ingram, and William de Somerville.
